explanation.rule: Prefixes are added to the beginning of words to change or reverse their meaning.

groups.note: Gives the opposite meaning or shows reversal
Means 'again' — doing something a second time

examples.definition: to put something back or substitute

wordForms.example: The magician made the coin disappear in an instant.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to become visible or to arrive

wordForms.definition: to give something and receive something else in return
wordForms.example: They decided to exchange phone numbers after the meeting.
The teacher asked her to redo the exercise more carefully.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to perform an action or task

wordForms.definition: to put something back or substitute with something new
wordForms.example: We need to replace the broken window before winter.
The scenery at the top of the mountain was unbelievable.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to feel certain that something is true

wordForms.example: He has been unemployed since the factory closed.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to pay someone to do work

wordForms.definition: the state of not having paid work
wordForms.example: The government is trying to reduce youth unemployment.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to pay someone to do work

wordForms.definition: heavier than what is considered healthy or normal
wordForms.example: The doctor said the patient was slightly overweight.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to measure the heaviness of something

multipleChoiceExercises.sentence: The suspect seemed to ___ into the crowd and was never found.
The students had to ___ the faulty calculation from scratch.
The old light bulbs need to be ___ with energy-saving ones.
The result of the match was absolutely ___.
He has been ___ for six months and is struggling financially.
We could ___ tokens for prizes at the end of the event.

transformExercises.sentence: After the storm, the rainbow seemed to ___ just as suddenly. (APPEAR)
The teacher told him to ___ the test because his answers were rushed. (DO)
We need to ___ the old equipment before the season starts. (PLACE)
The view from the summit was absolutely ___. (BELIEVE)
High levels of ___ can cause serious problems for society. (EMPLOY)
Students had the opportunity to ___ their books at the library fair. (CHANGE)
